title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
input_row.description: Disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server.
input_row.rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests. These requests are then served by the Kubelet server. You should rely on authentication to authorize access and disallow anonymous requests.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false .
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/kubelet file on each node and set the KUBELET_ARGS parameter to "--anonymous-auth=false" : KUBELET_ARGS="--anonymous-auth=false" Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl restart kubelet.service Impact: Anonymous requests will be rejected.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kubelet-authentication-authorization/#kubelet- authentication 149 | P a g e
input_row.default_value: By default, anonymous access is enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_anonymous_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the Kubelet configuration.
- Specifically, verify the `--anonymous-auth` flag in the Kubelet's configuration.
- The command to retrieve the Kubelet configuration can be executed as follows:
  ```bash
  ps -ef | grep kubelet
  ```
- Check the output for the presence of `--anonymous-auth=false`.
- **Pass Criteria:** The output must include `--anonymous-auth=false`.
- **Fail Criteria:** The output does not include `--anonymous-auth=false` or includes `--anonymous-auth=true`.
